page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-metal is a bare-metal server configured with 288 vCPUs across 144 physical Intel Xeon 6985P-C cores. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, the processor features hyper-threading and a 504 MB L3 cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The system is equipped with 2232 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz, yielding 7.75 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 313,057.86 and a PassMark CPU score of 179,028.27. Its memory subsystem delivers high throughput, with uncached read bandwidth reaching 1090.07 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 412.54 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a PassMark database score of 40,021.03 and Redis SET performance of 423.02 million operations per second. While the server excels in CPU-bound tasks like software compilation and ray tracing, its LLM inference text generation speeds are more modest, particularly for smaller models where it registers 42.98 tokens per second.

The bare-metal architecture of this instance eliminates hypervisor overhead, maximizing the qualitative cost efficiency of its massive 288-core compute and 2.2 TB memory footprint. The absence of bundled local storage requires external block storage attachment, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. This server is optimized for memory-intensive, highly parallelized enterprise workloads. Suitable use cases include large-scale relational databases, in-memory data grids, high-performance computing (HPC) simulations, software compilation pipelines, and heavy multi-threaded data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 288 vCPUs (144 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors running at 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 2232 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 RAM, providing 7.75 GB of memory per core, but lacks local storage and GPUs. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth (1090.07 GB/sec uncached read), and database operations, achieving 423.02 million Redis SET operations per second. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This configuration is highly efficient for large-scale, memory-intensive enterprise workloads, such as in-memory databases, heavy data processing, and high-performance computing, where hypervisor overhead must be eliminated.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-metal is a bare-metal server with 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2232 GB of DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-throughput database operations and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2232 GB DDR5 RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server combining 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs with 2.2 TB of DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 288 vCPUs (144 physical cores) Intel Xeon 6985P-C at 4.2 GHz.
Equipped with 2232 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 RAM.
Delivers top-tier database performance with 423.02 million Redis SET operations per second.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math.
Bare-metal architecture eliminates hypervisor overhead for maximum resource density.
Best suited for in-memory databases, software compilation, and large-scale data processing.
